classes.reasoning: This document explicitly self-identifies as 'RESUME' of a clinical study protocol. The cover page states 'Essai de phase III, national, multicentrique, prospectif, randomisé, en double aveugle, contrôlé contre placebo' and lists version history, protocol numbers, CPP approval, and ANSM authorization. It contains comprehensive protocol sections including objectives, inclusion/exclusion criteria, methodology, and study schema—all hallmarks of a clinical study protocol synopsis.

trial_title: Efficacité du sildénafil sur la distance de claudication absolue chez les patients atteints d'artériopathie oblitérante des membres inférieurs et présentant une claudication intermittente
intervention: Citrate de sildénafil (sildenafil citrate) 140 mg/jour (equivalent to 100 mg sildenafil base) versus placebo
indication: Artériopathie oblitérante des membres inférieurs (AOMI) / peripheral arterial occlusive disease with intermittent claudication (claudication intermittente)
summary: This is a protocol synopsis for VIRTUOSE, a phase III, national, multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial evaluating the efficacy of sildenafil on absolute claudication distance in patients with peripheral arterial occlusive disease (AOMI) and intermittent claudication. The trial compares daily oral sildenafil 100 mg (140 mg citrate) versus placebo for 24 weeks, with follow-up to week 48, in 220 patients. The primary endpoint is the absolute difference in maximum walking distance at week 24. The sponsor is CHU de Rennes, and the coordinating investigator is Dr. Loukman Omarjee.
references_to: Avis favorable le 12/10/2020 du CPP Ile-de-France 8 (Boulogne-Billancourt)
